Metadata.homeDescription: Fullstack developer available for hire. I build modern web applications, APIs, and SaaS products with React, Next.js, and Node.js. Based in Brazil, working with clients worldwide.

content.name: Nélio Espíndula Junior
content.headline: Hello, I'm
content.intro: I build web applications that are fast, maintainable, and focused on user experience. If you need a reliable developer to bring your product to life, you're in the right place.
content.about: I hold a degree in Computer Science and work as a fullstack developer, balancing thoughtful interfaces with solid, well-organized backend systems. I believe in steady progress, clear code, and solutions that prioritize simplicity, maintainability, and good digital experiences — for both users and the teams that maintain them.

sections.contactDescription: I'm currently available for new projects. Whether you need a web application built from scratch, a feature added to an existing product, or technical guidance — reach out and let's talk.

services.name: Fullstack Web Development
services.description: Complete web applications from database design to user interface, built with React, Next.js, and Node.js.

services.name: API Design & Integration
services.description: REST APIs, third-party integrations, payment gateways, and data pipelines designed for reliability and clarity.

services.name: SaaS Product Development
services.description: Multi-tenant platforms with subscription management, authentication flows, and admin dashboards — like LabAssyst, built from scratch.

services.name: Performance & Technical SEO
services.description: Core Web Vitals optimization, server-side rendering, and SEO-ready architecture for applications that need to rank and convert.

projects.name: Zig Tickets
projects.description: I worked as a fullstack developer on the product's development and evolution, integrating APIs, building intuitive interfaces, and ensuring performance and reliability in ticket sales and validation flows.

projects.name: LabAssyst
projects.description: I built a complete Laboratory Information System for small and mid-sized labs in a multi-tenant SaaS model focused on usability and quality.

projects.name: TISS xml
projects.description: A small project to create and generate the ANS standard for electronic healthcare data exchange, TISS, making document creation more dynamic and accessible.

listing.title: Writing archive
listing.description: A personal log of ideas, experiments, and questions.

posts.title: Building UX That Feels Intentional
posts.summary: Small interface choices that quietly shape how people feel while using a product.

blocks.text: Most users cannot name why an interface feels right, but they notice it quickly. Good UX often comes from reducing friction and keeping a clear rhythm.
An intentional interface gives people a path. It avoids noise, respects attention, and behaves in ways users can predict.

items: Use motion only when it helps understanding.

blocks.text: People forgive minimal visuals. They do not forgive confusion.

posts.title: My Next.js Folder Structure for Real Projects
posts.summary: A structure that stays calm as projects grow.

blocks.text: Folder structure is not about being clever. It is about reducing hesitation when you return to the code later.

items: Route-specific logic stays close to the route.
Reusable parts live in obvious shared places.
Names explain intention, not technical trivia.

blocks.text: The goal is not perfect organization. The goal is easy maintenance.

posts.title: Frontend Performance Without Guessing
posts.summary: A simple loop for performance work that avoids random optimizations.

blocks.text: Performance gets expensive when teams optimize blindly. Measure, change, verify, repeat.

items: Create a baseline for key pages.
Retest every change and keep notes.

blocks.text: This keeps performance work calm and grounded.
